sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.19 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Suppose that$S$and$M$are groups of finite Morley rank,$S$is an infinite group, and$M$is a non-trivial connected group definably and faithfully acting on~$S$. This action is said to be if$M$does not leave invariant any definable non-trivial proper subgroup of$S$. Prove that if$S$is a simple group such that every proper definable subgroup of$S$is nilpotent, and the action of$M$on$S$is irreducible, t...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
